reviews.review_title: Rare Work of Art - And Fun!
reviews.review_description: First and foremost, this is an art-first experience. Yes, it is fun, puzzles, and adventure. But it is not only one of the most beautiful games I've seen in VR (and I've seen a lot), it is one of the most artistic games I've seen in VR. Visiting this game is like visiting an art installation. It is really an unusual experience. If you enjoy art, games, puzzles, and/or adventure, just go buy this game. Another unusual aspect of the game is the re-directed walking that allows you to stay in a small area. Very few other games do this: Hotel Infinity, TraVRsal, Tea For God, Eye of the Temple,  Sommad, and Exit Condition One. I have to say, The Amusement  is one of the best of these. Hats off to the developers of this game.

Could not wait to jump in, and wow already have that butterfly feeling, it’s so clever, works in 2 x 2 space and works just as well in VR I’m physically turning but using motion control.
Another superb puzzle story adventure to add to my list.
Just outstanding.
Now completed the game, the story was so heartwarming & heartfelt, it will stay with me for a long time.i could have played for hours more as the game had me gripped from start to finish.
A true puzzle masterpiece.

Normally this would be an easy 5* review. The game itself is gorgeous. If you have the space, this is simply a must buy. BUT. There is no way to move the playarea. The only way I could get it kind of working was by drawing a 2x2 area, but it was still wonky. I can't believe such an important feature is missing (or really, really well hidden)

developer_response.body: Hi, thanks for your review! Sorry to hear that you had problems with the playspace. We just released a patch today (v1.1.33) that introduces a new feature to fix your problem. We've made setting up your play space much more flexible: You can now move and rotate your virtual play space to better match your real-world space.

reviews.review_title: A great adventure with a few issues.
reviews.review_description:
After shelving this for a little while I just got back to it and played it to completion.

Luckily the hint frequency continued to decrease and some puzzles had me thinking for a bit to solve, so as the game progressed it became more challenging.

Unfortunately I hit a few puzzle glitches that I had to reload checkpoints to get past.

Also I ran into a glitch that caused the world to shake and jitter around for a few seconds. Luckily I only hit the bug once.

Overall it's a great adventure in beautiful environments and one of the best examples of redirected walking I've run across.

Highly recommended!

Original Review:

I'd love to give this game a five star rating but it has one very serious flaw.

The puzzle hints cannot be switched off.

What's even worse is that the hints come almost immediately. It's very frustrating to be told the solution to a puzzle when you've just seen it seconds earlier. Frustration in a puzzle game should come from facing challenging puzzles, not because the game won't let you solve them and insists on giving you the solutions.

Hints should always be optional in a puzzle game and it's astonishing that the developers didn't realize this.

Luckily it seems the hints are getting less frequent as the game progresses but unfortunately some puzzles still solve themselves even several chapters into the game.

So it's not a great puzzle game.

However if I look at it as just an interactive story it's exceptional.

The art direction is fantastic. It's always a joy to be in an environment designed by competent artists.

However there are a few technical issues.

Object handling can be a bit janky. They can get stuck in scenery or other objects, occasionally making a puzzle difficult to complete. Luckily this doesn't happen too often.

I've played most of the "redirected walking" games for the Quest and this game makes excellent use of the technology, but like any redirected walking game you can occasionally run into issues with the spatial positioning. I've played through a few chapters and I've only had to quit the game and relaunch it once so far.

You can also play it with standard smooth locomotion or teleportation controls but for the most immersive experience playing with redirected walking is the way to go.

If they add a setting to disable the hints this'll be a five star game, but it'll be too late for me because I can't stop playing it because of the wonderful atmosphere and art direction.

This game is set in a very old amusement park that you are sent to investigate.  I won't spoil any more but there is a very compelling story to keep you playing.  The story is told through pop up style animation and the voice acting is well done too. The game is about 3 or so hours long and will keep you engaged the whole way.  The coolest part of the game is the way it uses room scale to get you more immersed in the surroundings.  I just wish it was a bit longer because I would have liked another 2 hours of story.

reviews.review_title: It is worthy of the title !
reviews.review_description: I almost did not purchase this due to the free roam tea for God type locomotion as I always play my game sitting down in a chair and I move with a joystick. It took me a while to confirm, but you can absolutely play sitting with regular smooth locomotion and I did. I personally think this would have been better if the more standard approach was taken it could have had such a huge epic scale to explore and walk amongst but I was impressed with how much that was perceived considering you're always in a tiny little space in this game. It still gave me that story driven narrative puzzle adventure that I play VR for in the first place. There was some slight jankiness however it did not break immersion and for a first day release you really can't expect perfection and this was close. I was completely immersed in the story, was excited to solve the mystery, and it was one of those games that you buy, and you just keep playing till the end because you don't want to stop that's what I look for when purchasing games and this gave it to me. The mission on the ferris wheel.... To the devs... bravo! I am not afraid of heights in VR but somehow after completing that level I felt like I just got off a ride the tension and apprehension of climbing out completely immersed me as if it was really happening that is hard to do and it was worth the price of the game right there.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for this feedback! Really happy that the story and these special moments worked out so well for you :)
We tried to make the game as most enjoyable as possible also for seated playing and teleportation / smooth locomotion. With the patch that we just released today (v.1.1.33) we even improved seated mode, and some low-to-the-ground hoses are now easier to grab.

reviews.review_description:
I found myself getting the “out of boundaries” message a lot, it made the game unplayable for a good chunk of the sections due to the weird movement settings. I wanted to like it more but I feel like it needs some more work. 

I tried playing it as intended by standing in a large play area but as a tall person it felt off moving through mazes. 

I hate to give such a low score, but I’ll keep at it and update this review upon beating the game.

Love the art design. It's a dreamy turn of the century atmosphere. The graphics are great und riddles use physics nicely.
It reminds me of the oculus cv1 game called Torn, that was a great game too. 
I love it when a game captures the beauty of the past. Especially in VR. 5 stars from me.

Holy smokes this may be one of the most well crafted games I have played. Everything is designed so beautifully, the voice acting and the surround sound are so good. 
If you have 2x2 metres of space and like some slow paced a little artsy and unbelievably atmospheric games this is a dream for you.

What a brilliant game! Graphic is beautiful, world building is detailed and immersive, and the story is very emotional and touching. Redirected walking is a brilliant use of VR. Although it does occasionally get a bit disorienting for me. E.g. at the maze level in the beginning, (after all you are physically walking in circles), but it is the most immersive way of moving in the virtual world I have ever experienced. Overall fantastic experience.

The Amusement isn’t just a game—it’s a rare work of art. From its dreamy, turn-of-the-century aesthetic to its heartwarming narrative, this is one of the most visually stunning and emotionally gripping experiences in VR. If you love puzzles, adventure, and immersive storytelling, this is a must-play.
The art direction is breathtaking, evoking the charm of classic games like Torn while feeling entirely fresh. The physics-based puzzles are clever and satisfying, and the redirected walking (a rare feature in VR) lets you explore a vast world in just 2x2 meters of space—one of the best implementations of this tech yet.
The story is unforgettable, blending whimsy and depth in a way that lingers long after the credits roll. Whether you play seated with smooth locomotion or room-scale with physical movement, the experience adapts seamlessly. The voice acting and sound design pull you deeper into its world, making every moment feel alive.
A few minor jinks (now improved in the latest patch) don’t detract from the sheer magic of this game. The studio is still actively working on the game and improving it which is always a good sign. The Ferris wheel sequence alone is worth the price of admission—tense, immersive, and brilliantly executed.
Final Verdict: If you love art, puzzles, or VR that feels like a living painting, The Amusement is a masterpiece you won’t want to end. 10/10—support this kind of creativity!

Get it!! I wish it wouldn’t ever end to b honest , even w puzzles being closer to easy then hard,the rare high quality, artistic visuals and very good story makes up for all! If u r a puzzler most def inatly recommend hit up for a code if need one also cau will send Asa I see it and play daily and with a discount to the already fair price it is without it honestly looks like we robbed them then😅 yep, it’s that good😅 Can’t wait to see what all they games they do next! Ty for a beautiful experience and keep them coming ! Big supporter here and  the discount offer isn’t about me making the money it’s so u definatly will get it w that discounted price so others don’t hav excuse to to see this work
Of art  and gem they made this game to be🥰

Beautifully done! Excellent graphics, narrative, gameplay, and variety. I played seated with joystick controls and everything worked great. The graphics and story are amazing! I didn’t want it to end. Thank you developers for an excellent experience.I have to add that the puzzles are great!

You physically walk through a gorgeous environment while solving relatively easy puzzles with an interesting narrative to tie it all together. I wish more games would experiment with this movement style. It’s the most immersive VR experience you can have IMHO

I have never been so frustrated with a game as this one my left hand will not track properly and every time i take my headset of for a break when returning to the game it wants me to stand in a totally different boundary setting which is a different room to the boundary that i original set the game and graphics up to now have been good but im slowly starting to drift away from enjoying this game shame.

developer_response.body: We are sorry about your problems with the boundary. Re-setting of position when taking off the headset is a behaviour of the SDK we cannot really change. In most games, it doesn't stand out but because we are using a true room-scale approach it stands out clearly. Unfortunately, you need to re-set the boundaries in this case. (or use teleportation/smooth locomotion where this does not matter)

reviews.review_description:
UPDATE :
OK, I've just updated to 5 stars having played more of it. But play on a swivel stool for the best experience. 

It's a very good game, but there are some issues with the boundary,, sometimes getting stuck outside and not able to get back. I changed to seated and used a swivel stool and smooth movement with joystick and it is far better, though occasionally I got stuck there too and had to restart. It has a nice storyline, the graphics and mechanics are excellent, but again you have to be careful not to go past the boundaries.. When the view goes from grey to black, you're going to get stuck. You can see the footprints of where to go but it often doesn't work.. So my advice,, stay well within boundaries to prevent frustration. I think the game would have been better with more traditional movement and I think anyone seated in a non movable chair is not going to have a pleasant experience.. The best way to play is a swivel stool rather than walking around the 2x2m space. I do recommend it, but it needs some improvement particularly on the boundaries, they're far too tight.

Good story. Bad voice acting (except for the mother). Easy puzzles. Short game. I didn’t think the environments were anything special to look at. Nice job with the vertigo and claustrophobia (in the elevators).

The first game in a while that lives up to the potential of Quest 3's graphics. A quality and fun adventure in an atmospheric seaside setting that's reminiscent of Bioshock Infinite. And similar in quality to the excellent Ghost Town.

reviews.review_title: Fantastic experience and a must buy imho
reviews.review_description: Wow just wow this is truly what vr is about and sets it apart from other games , brillant directing and redirecting of ur walking in a contain 6 by 6ft play space. Graphically stunningly breathtakingly beautiful and fantastically immersive with the walking,  it feels so real , I'd give it 10 stars if I could

I have really enjoyed this game, It’s got this weird, slightly creepy atmosphere that pulls you in right away, and I found myself wanting to keep going just to figure out what was actually happening. It doesn’t hand things to you, which I liked—but , it can also feel a bit confusing at times. Overall, I’d say it’s worth checking out and one for the library

This game is halfway between a work of art and a traditional video game. Some levels are very beautiful and visually enjoyable, while others feel too close to standard game mechanics, which actually trivializes the experience and takes away enjoyment rather than adding to it.

A future update with a more careful focus on the aesthetics of certain levels would be very welcome.

There are rooms that are visually very beautiful, full of objects and interesting details, but they only appear as decorative side areas: you cannot enter or interact with them, while you remain confined to an area that feels more like a classic platform-style video game.

Although the puzzles are simple, which is fine, making them long only creates boredom. For example, I found myself facing a wall where I had to repeatedly move a magnetic ball with a wand from one point to another many times, without requiring any real skill—just constant repetition until it became completely boring.

So if you want to keep a very simple “puzzle” like that, at least make it short, and not something that requires no ability while also becoming tedious through repetition.

Also, now that I have progressed further in the game and am almost at the end, I realized that it would have been better to have more sections of natural walking movement, whereas instead there are many climbing sections, and in the end you actually walk very little.

One final note: please make the dialogue skippable, because it is completely boring and unnecessary for non-native English speakers.

Finally, given that with artificial intelligence it is now extremely easy to produce high-quality audio at very low cost—or sometimes even for free—it is not clear why other languages, including Italian, are not included. Since this is a narrative adventure, being able to hear it in one’s own language would make the experience far more rewarding.

Not only is this missing, but even text subtitles in different languages are absent, and again, using AI it would have been very easy to implement them.
thaks🤞👋

developer_response.body: Thanks for your review! I'm sorry that there is no italian language support. Having high-quality voice overs from real actors is quite laborious and therefore we decided to only have english voices. However, there are subtitles in 5 different languages (english, french, german, spanish, japanese), and maybe we will add more languages in the future.

reviews.review_description: Everything I love in a puzzle game: incredible stereoscopic graphics, great fantasy adventure and puzzles that have just the right amount of difficulty... I always managed to figure them out without having to find a "walk through" video. Loved it!
Great graphics, story and interesting gameplay.  Not to mention very artistic design. This is an easy five stars!
Love these roomscale style games where you travel around your room and it feels like you're walking around an entire world. The inclusion of Quest Achievements is greatly appreciated!

I am enjoying playing this narrative puzzler, although it does get a bit repetitive at times. The climbing sections start to get a bit boring after a while, as do the repeated mechanics of moving the hook, inserting batteries, grappling, etc. I love narrative driven games, but the story in this one is not really grabbing me for some reason. I’m planning to finish it, but I guess I was expecting something slightly different with the amusement park theme. Happy to support these devs though. We need more experiences like this. Loved the walking parts as well!

This game is pretty great! Granted I'm only through with the first section but just everything about this is oozing Style. Can't wait to see what's ahead in this game and then what's in the future for the Developers

A beautifully crafted VR narrative experience with strong atmosphere and innovative movement system. The physical exploration adds a deep sense of immersion. A memorable and artistic journey. A jewel!
This is what VR games should be. Very good use of the VR technology. Interesting story without being overwhelming. I very much hope there will be more games like this one.
It was amazing and the plot is one that left me speechless which hasn't happened in a while. A truly fun game 10 outta 10
Great game! I just wish the sky wasnt completly still. It gives a weird vibe.

Get it! It is awsome! Everything I wanted in a vr game! Worth every penny!  
You wont be disapointed 10 star!!!!

reviews.review_title: Hidden gem. Room scale at its best!
reviews.review_description: Wonderful story, visuals that impress, sound design that creates atmosphere. It is a solid package. Add to this wonderful use of room scale exploring and movment and you have an outstanding narrative driven adventure story that should be in everyone’s collection. Highly recommended.

I wasn't really prepared for what this game offered. Been vr gaming for along time and this the first game I come across which makes you feel like you are walking around freely, even though your in a 2x2m square.

It very cleverly thought out and with clever use of re direction. It must of been an absolute headache developing and testing though. If you don't play it this way, you are missing out on a huge part of the experience.

As for the game? The puzzles are ok, but not very taxing and can get abit repetitive as time goes on as there is not much variety in them. Also the amount of climbing can be abit to much later on to.

But seeing that it's from a small development team these can be forgiven. For the content and narrative it offered, was about the right length.

If you like puzzle/narrative games and have the room, it's a must buy.
